test.name: Structured Output Compliance
test.description: Produce JSON matching an exact schema with no extra fields
test.prompt:
List exactly 7 programming languages that were first released after the year 2010.

You MUST follow this exact format for each item:
<number>. <LanguageName> (<year>) - <one sentence description>

Rules:
1. List EXACTLY 7 languages — not 6, not 8.
2. Each language must have been first released (v1.0 or initial public release) AFTER 2010, meaning 2011 or later.
3. Each item must be on its own line.
4. The format must be exactly: number, period, space, language name, space, year in parentheses, space, dash, space, one sentence description.
5. Each description must be exactly ONE sentence (one period at the end, no additional sentences).
6. Do NOT include any text before the list (no introduction, no "Here are..." preamble).
7. Do NOT include any text after the list (no summary, no notes, no caveats).
8. Output ONLY the 7 numbered items, nothing else.

Example of the correct format (do NOT use this language, it's just to show format):
1. ExampleLang (2015) - A language designed for example purposes only.

claude-opus-4-8.modelOutput:
1. Kotlin (2011) - A statically typed language for the JVM and Android that emphasizes conciseness and null safety.
2. Dart (2011) - A client-optimized language developed by Google for building cross-platform apps, notably with Flutter.
3. Elixir (2012) - A functional, concurrent language built on the Erlang virtual machine for scalable and fault-tolerant systems.
4. TypeScript (2012) - A typed superset of JavaScript that compiles to plain JavaScript for safer large-scale development.
5. Julia (2012) - A high-performance dynamic language designed primarily for numerical and scientific computing.
6. Swift (2014) - A modern compiled language created by Apple for building iOS, macOS, and other Apple platform applications.
7. Rust (2015) - A systems programming language focused on memory safety and concurrency without a garbage collector.

claude-sonnet-4-6.modelOutput:
1. Kotlin (2011) - A statically typed, JVM-compatible language developed by JetBrains as a more concise and safe alternative to Java.
2. Dart (2011) - A client-optimized language created by Google for building fast, cross-platform mobile and web applications.
3. TypeScript (2012) - A strongly typed superset of JavaScript developed by Microsoft that compiles to plain JavaScript.
4. Julia (2012) - A high-performance, dynamically typed language designed specifically for numerical and scientific computing.
5. Elixir (2012) - A functional, concurrent language built on the Erlang VM, designed for scalable and fault-tolerant distributed systems.
6. Swift (2014) - A compiled, statically typed language created by Apple as a modern replacement for Objective-C in iOS and macOS development.
7. Zig (2016) - A low-level systems programming language focused on robustness, performance, and explicit memory management without hidden control flow.

gpt-5-5.modelOutput:
1. Dart (2011) - A client-focused language from Google for building web, server, and mobile applications.
2. Elixir (2011) - A functional language built on the Erlang VM for scalable and fault-tolerant systems.
3. Julia (2012) - A high-performance language designed for numerical computing, data science, and scientific research.
4. TypeScript (2012) - A statically typed superset of JavaScript that compiles to plain JavaScript.
5. Hack (2014) - A language developed by Meta that adds static typing and other features to PHP.
6. Swift (2014) - An Apple-developed language for building apps across iOS, macOS, and related platforms.
7. Crystal (2014) - A compiled language with Ruby-inspired syntax and static type checking.

grok.modelOutput:
1. Kotlin (2011) - A statically typed programming language developed by JetBrains that targets the Java Virtual Machine and offers seamless interoperability with Java.
2. Dart (2011) - An object-oriented programming language created by Google for building fast and smooth client applications across web, mobile, and desktop platforms.
3. Elixir (2012) - A dynamic functional programming language built on the Erlang virtual machine and designed for creating scalable and fault-tolerant distributed systems.
4. Julia (2012) - A high-level high-performance dynamic programming language created for technical and scientific computing that delivers the speed of C combined with Python-like usability.
5. TypeScript (2012) - A typed superset of JavaScript developed by Microsoft that adds optional static typing and other features while compiling to plain JavaScript.
6. Swift (2014) - A general-purpose compiled programming language developed by Apple to provide a safer and more expressive alternative to Objective-C for Apple platform development.
7. Rust (2015) - A systems programming language focused on memory safety without a garbage collector along with high performance and fearless concurrency that reached its first stable release in 2015.
